resultRows.sourceLabel: CDC Genetic Testing; MedlinePlus Genetics; MedlinePlus Genetic Counseling
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: A qualified professional needs validated test scope, personal and family history, symptoms, medications, labs, procedure context, environment, ancestry applicability, and current clinical information before using genetic context for health decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The cover and table of contents identify a Healthcare Pro sample report with sections for STAT Alerts, Increased Risk Detected, Medications (Pharmacogenomics), Rare Diseases, No Increased Risk Detected, and Analysis Alerts.
resultRows.description: This row captures sample-report structure only and does not prove a local Healthcare Pro report was generated.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 1-2 of the public sample PDF; keep report identity and section list separate from local findings.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Nitrous Oxide ADR as Risk Detected and ties the alert to homozygous MTHFR Trp339Gly/W339G.
resultRows.description: This is a sample-person alert row only; local output must not infer nitrous oxide adverse-reaction risk, homocysteine status, anesthesia clearance, or medical-record action without supplied evidence and professional review.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public sample PDF; sample clinical-action language is observed structure, not local advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels CAD & MI as Increased Risk Detected and shows a 55% lifetime-risk sample estimate compared with a 42% male population-risk label.
resultRows.description: This is sample output only; local output must not reuse the percentages as a personal coronary disease or heart-attack risk estimate.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 4-5 of the public sample PDF; percentages are sample labels and need calibrated local evidence before reuse.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels MTHFR Deficiency as Increased Risk Detected due to homozygous MTHFR Trp339Gly/W339G.
resultRows.description: This is sample-person structure only; local output must not infer deficiency, homocysteine levels, supplementation need, or reproductive guidance from the sample row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 5 of the public sample PDF; preserve the sample label without turning it into local clinical guidance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Colorectal Cancer as Increased Risk Detected and shows a sample lifetime-risk label of about 15% compared with about 7%.
resultRows.description: This is sample output only; local output must not infer personal colorectal cancer risk, screening timing, aspirin use, or prevention steps.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 5 of the public sample PDF; sample prevention text is not local medical advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Osteoarthritis as Increased Risk Detected and lists knee OA moderate, hip OA slightly increased, and wrist OA normal sample labels.
resultRows.description: This row captures the sample's joint-specific output shape only; local output must not infer personal osteoarthritis risk or exercise modifications from this sample.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 5-6 of the public sample PDF; exercise and imaging language is observed sample structure only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Hirschsprung Disease as Increased Risk Detected and ties the row to a RET mutation described as IVS 1 +9277 and +9349 relative to MET.
resultRows.description: This is a sample-person rare-disease risk row only; local output must not infer diagnosis, symptoms, imaging need, or referral need.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 7 of the public sample PDF; symptom and referral examples are sample report structure, not local advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Thrombophilia as Carrier and states the sample has one copy of the Factor V R2 mutation in F5, distinct from Factor V Leiden.
resultRows.description: This is sample carrier-status structure only; local output must not infer personal thrombophilia carrier status or clotting risk.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 8 of the public sample PDF; the row includes sample caveats about R2 on its own.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Warfarin dosing as Slight Resistance Detected and says the sample may need a slightly higher maintenance dose.
resultRows.description: This is sample pharmacogenomics structure only; local output must not infer a warfarin dose, INR target, prescription change, or medication action.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 9 of the public sample PDF; medication-dose language must remain sample-only unless supplied by validated local PGx evidence and professional review.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels 5-fluorouracil toxicity as increased risk due to DPYD*5/*5 and states the sample is homozygous for DPYD I543V.
resultRows.description: This is sample pharmacogenomics structure only; local output must not infer chemotherapy toxicity risk, alternative therapy, or dose adjustment.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 10 of the public sample PDF; oncology medication action requires qualified clinical review.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report labels Hemochromatosis as Carrier and ties the carrier row to a single HFE2 G320V mutation.
resultRows.description: This is sample carrier-status structure only; local output must not infer iron overload, carrier status, family risk, monitoring, or treatment.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 11 of the public sample PDF; monitoring and phlebotomy language is sample content, not local guidance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report's Analysis Alerts section states no ambiguity, complete genetic data, and all diseases, traits, and conditions analyzed.
resultRows.description: This is sample analysis-quality language only; local output must not claim complete local data or all conditions analyzed unless the local fixture supplies equivalent quality evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 14 of the public sample PDF; data-completeness claims are sample-only.

professionalHandoffContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Healthcare Pro is modeled as a local handoff-boundary package with observed public sample-report structure. It can document supplied evidence, sample-only rows, and missing professional context, but it cannot replace qualified review or produce medical decisions from local evidence alone.
unsupportedClaims: Diagnosis, professional clearance, all-clear result, or personal disease-risk estimate
Medication choice, dosing, procedure, treatment, supplement, diet, lab, screening, reproductive, lifestyle, prevention, or management instruction
Variant classification, penetrance, inheritance, family-member risk, clinical actionability, or specialist-level interpretation
Reuse of sample genotypes, carrier labels, medication labels, percentages, or analysis-completeness statements as local findings
professionalHandoffContext.professionalReview: Use this output only as preparation for a professional conversation. Health decisions require qualified review with clinical history, medications, symptoms, labs, family history, procedure details when relevant, and validated testing scope.

professionalReview: Do not use this local fixture to change medication, supplements, diet, screening, procedures, reproductive decisions, prevention plans, or treatment decisions.

Bring current symptoms, personal and family history, medications, allergies, labs, prior testing, procedure context when relevant, and ancestry context to a qualified professional if discussing this topic.
Treat sample rows as examples of report format, not as normal, abnormal, low risk, high risk, reassuring, diagnostic, actionable, or all-clear local results.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report uses this RET row for a Hirschsprung Disease increased-risk sample label.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local Hirschsprung Disease risk, diagnosis, symptoms, imaging, or referral need.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report labels the sample as a Factor V R2 carrier and distinguishes it from Factor V Leiden.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local thrombophilia carrier status or clotting risk.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report labels possible increased Mercaptopurine and Azathioprine adverse-reaction risk due to an ITPA mutation.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report labels increased 5-fluorouracil toxicity risk due to homozygous DPYD I543V.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local chemotherapy toxicity risk, alternative-agent need, or dosing guidance.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report labels the sample as a postanesthetic-apnea carrier and likely not affected.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report labels the sample as a Hemochromatosis carrier due to HFE2 G320V.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local hemochromatosis carrier status, iron overload, monitoring, or treatment need.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report states its own analysis completeness and lack-of-ambiguity alerts on the final page.

limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, and complete callset files are not included.

The report is educational handoff context and does not provide diagnosis, professional clearance, disease-risk estimation, medication guidance, procedure guidance, treatment advice, supplement advice, diet advice, lab interpretation, screening advice, reproductive advice, or clinical actionability.
Sample rows should never be interpreted as personal low risk, high risk, no risk, normal, abnormal, reassuring, diagnostic, actionable, or all clear results.
